global_policy.access_scope: Read only publisher-advertised public Atom or JSON API endpoints; never scrape article HTML as fallback.
global_policy.attribution: Every displayed signal names the publishing organisation, links its exact source_url, and shows the source-specific attribution label, terms link, and notice.
global_policy.breaking_format_change: Fail closed, mark only that source degraded, keep other sources running, and require fixture plus catalog review before accepting the new format.
global_policy.removal: Disable fetching and public display immediately when the publisher withdraws the feed or reuse permission, access becomes authenticated or prohibited, mapping becomes unsafe, or attribution can no longer be preserved. Historical source metadata may remain only for internal audit.
global_policy.content_policy: Store title-derived metadata and only the bounded fields approved per source. Do not mirror article bodies, raw payloads, images, logos, personal data, comments, third-party works, or imply publisher endorsement.

sources.reuse_policy: GOV.UK public-sector information is reusable under the Open Government Licence v3.0, including commercial and adapted use, subject to attribution and exclusions.
reuse_requirements: Show Innovate UK as the publisher and link the original GOV.UK entry.
Link the Open Government Licence v3.0 where practical and indicate that the digest is a normalized summary.
Do not reuse excluded logos, personal data, third-party works, or imply official endorsement.

request_policy.rate_limit: at most one request per 60 minutes
request_policy.redirect_policy: maximum 3 redirects; final URL must remain HTTPS on www.gov.uk

sources.accepted_item_policy: Accept only a headline that names one company before an explicit raises, secures, launches, wins, or receives event verb. Skip programme announcements, aggregate project lists, policy, advice, recruitment, reports, and articles without one unambiguous company subject.

startup_name.rule: Extract the company text before an approved concrete-event verb; never take a programme, department, person, or consortium name.

canonical_url.rule: The Atom entry does not expose a structured company homepage; never treat the GOV.UK article as the startup canonical URL.

source_url.rule: Require an absolute HTTPS www.gov.uk URL and retain it exactly for OGL attribution.

signal_type.rule: raises/secures/receives with explicit capital maps to funding; launches maps to launch; wins maps to award; other admitted concrete company events map to news.

published_at.rule: Parse RFC 3339 with timezone and convert to UTC; never substitute fetch time.

description.rule: Strip markup, decode entities, collapse whitespace, and keep at most 280 Unicode characters of the feed-provided summary.

region.rule: A GOV.UK publication does not prove company incorporation or operating region.

categories.rule: This organization feed has no stable product taxonomy; leave empty until a reviewed allowlist exists.

funding.rule: Populate amount, currency, and round only when explicitly present in the headline; investors remain empty.

raw_payload.rule: The adapter leaves RawPayload empty; NormalizeSignal derives bounded categories/funding JSON from the typed record and stores no raw feed body.

fixture_expected.description: Northstar Robotics develops inspection systems for offshore infrastructure.

fallback_policy.on_failure: mark this source degraded and continue other sources
fallback_policy.retry: next scheduled cadence with bounded backoff

sources.accepted_item_policy: Accept only a single named spinout or company with a concrete launch, funding, award, acquisition, or commercial milestone in the headline. Skip research policy, university aggregates, grants without a named company, events, reports, jobs, and consortium-only announcements.

startup_name.rule: Extract one company or spinout before an approved concrete-event verb; reject ambiguous institution and consortium headlines.

canonical_url.rule: The Atom entry has no structured company homepage; leave empty.

source_url.rule: Require an absolute HTTPS www.gov.uk URL and retain it exactly for OGL attribution.

signal_type.rule: raises/secures/receives with explicit capital maps to funding; launches maps to launch; wins maps to award; an explicit acquisition maps to acquisition; other admitted events map to news.

published_at.rule: Parse RFC 3339 with timezone and convert to UTC; never substitute fetch time.

description.rule: Strip markup, decode entities, collapse whitespace, and keep at most 280 Unicode characters of the feed-provided summary.

region.rule: Publisher scope and university location do not prove company region.

categories.rule: The feed has no stable product taxonomy; leave empty until a reviewed allowlist exists.

funding.rule: Populate amount, currency, and round only when explicitly present in the headline; investors remain empty.

raw_payload.rule: The adapter leaves RawPayload empty; NormalizeSignal derives bounded categories/funding JSON from the typed record and stores no raw feed body.

startup_name.rule: Extract the company before an approved concrete-event verb; reject fund, scheme, lender, report, and portfolio names unless the subject is one operating company.

source_url.rule: Require an absolute HTTPS www.gov.uk URL and retain it exactly for OGL attribution.

categories.rule: The feed has no stable product taxonomy; leave empty until a reviewed allowlist exists.

funding.rule: Populate amount, currency, and round only when explicitly present in the headline; investors remain empty.

sources.reuse_policy: The official API publishes public Hacker News story metadata in near real time; the digest stores only bounded launch title metadata and links to the original discussion.
reuse_requirements: Show Hacker News as the source and link the original discussion.
Do not store submitter profiles, comments, story text, votes or raw API payloads.
Do not imply that Hacker News or Y Combinator endorses the digest.

request_policy.redirect_policy: no redirects; every API request remains HTTPS on hacker-news.firebaseio.com

sources.accepted_item_policy: Accept only a live story whose title begins with Show HN and identifies one bounded product name, optionally followed by an explicit dash-separated tagline. Skip sentence-like, first-person, dead, deleted and malformed items.

startup_name.rule: Extract one safe product name after Show HN and before an explicit tagline separator, or accept a tightly bounded bare product name.

canonical_url.rule: Retain only an absolute HTTPS product URL without userinfo; never fetch it.

source_url.rule: Build the exact HTTPS news.ycombinator.com discussion URL from the validated numeric item id.

signal_type.rule: Every admitted Show HN item maps to launch.

published_at.rule: Convert the positive Unix timestamp to UTC; never substitute fetch time.

description.rule: Use only the bounded plain-text tagline after the product name separator.

region.rule: A Show HN story does not prove company region.

categories.rule: The API exposes no reviewed stable product taxonomy.

funding.rule: A Show HN launch does not prove funding data.

raw_payload.rule: The adapter decodes only required item fields and stores no raw JSON, submitter, comment, vote or story text data.

fallback_policy.on_failure: mark this source degraded and continue other sources
fallback_policy.retry: next scheduled cadence with bounded backoff
